prevalence.source: Orphanet; HAE epidemiology reviews; Indian allergy/immunology center reports
prevalence.trend: increasing diagnosed prevalence with improved complement testing awareness

burden.dalyPerCapita: very low (rare) but high acute morbidity; risk of fatal laryngeal edema without on-demand therapy
burden.mortalityRate: Low with access to therapy; higher with delayed diagnosis

vulnerablePopulations: patients in remote areas without emergency therapy
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Recurrent angioedema without urticaria; low C4; low C1-inhibitor level/function; confirm with repeat tests and genetics when available

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; cascade testing when index case diagnosed
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; depends on access to on-demand and prophylactic therapies

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Complement testing and specialist referral concentrated in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: On-demand therapy (C1-INH or bradykinin antagonist where available), prophylaxis for frequent/severe attacks, trigger avoidance, emergency action plan
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis and limited availability/cost of specific HAE therapies
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Distinct biomarker-based diagnosis and major benefit from correct therapy once accessed
